area: MSC 20F65 / 20F10 / 20B22 (geometric group theory; word & conjugacy problems; infinite permutation groups) — Thompson–Stein–Bieri–Strebel groups of PL homeomorphisms with slopes 2^a3^b, class-transposition (RCWA) groups, topological full groups
hole_justification: Searches run: "Collatz conjecture Thompson's group"; "Belk Bleak Matucci rearrangement groups base change Collatz"; "Collatz Thompson site:mathoverflow.net"; "'Collatz' 'Thompson's group' embedding monoid piecewise linear arXiv"; "Stein-Thompson group T(2,3) slopes 2 and 3 breakpoints Z[1/6]"; "Guba Sapir diagram groups Stein x=x^2 x=x^3"; "'Bieri-Strebel' OR 'Stein group' OR 'F_{2,3}' Collatz conjugacy affine"; "Collatz 'topological full group' OR 'etale groupoid'"; "Collatz Cuntz algebra O_2"; "Applegate Lagarias '3x+1 semigroup' wild Farkas"; plus full-text greps of the downloaded PDFs. FOUND (the fringe): (1) Kohl, J. Group Theory 2017 — the one serious paper putting Collatz inside a (2,3)-Thompson-like simple group, backed by his decade-long RCWA/GAP program (active through arXiv:2409.13341, 2024); (2) Hines arXiv:2202.04443 — realizes Thompson's F as congruential functions from Collatz's ORIGINAL 1932 permutation via Girard's GoI (category-theoretic observation, no 3x+1 dynamics); (3) Mori arXiv:2411.08084 — Cuntz O_2 operator reformulation (adjacent, since V lives in O_2-land); (4) Stérin–Woods 2020 (already in our library) — base-conversion embedding, with the 3x+1-binary / x-div-2-base-30 dual FSTs. NOT FOUND anywhere: any application of the Thompson school's actual machinery (tree-pair/diagram-group normal forms, strand-diagram conjugacy, Bieri–Strebel conjugacy invariants, BNSR invariants, action-graph geometry, topological-full-group rigidity) to 3x+1 cycles or divergence. Kohl himself says his equivalences await techniques from another area. Also: the recollection in our task that Belk–Bleak–Matucci published on the "(2,3) base change" homeomorphism is NOT supported in print — I checked their publication lists, the Belk–Bleak RIMS survey (grep: no Collatz/Kohl/base-change hits), and rational-group papers; the closest real items are Bleak–Cameron–Olukoya (shift automorphisms = bi-synchronizing transducers) and Stérin–Woods. The (2,3)-group structure theory itself is exploding right now with zero Collatz contact: Hyde–Skipper–Zaremsky (May 2026) proved Stein's F_{2,3} does not embed in V; Li 2023 does Stein groups as topological full groups; Molyneux 2026 does tree pairs for algebraic Bieri–Strebel groups.
bridge: Objects: the forward Collatz branches t_0(x)=x/2, t_1(x)=(3x+1)/2 are elements of the affine part A = {x↦λx+c : λ∈⟨2,3⟩, c∈Z[1/6]} of the Bieri–Strebel group G(R; Z[1/6], ⟨2,3⟩) — the line version of Stein's F_{2,3}. Maps: parity vectors w∈{0,1}^k correspond bijectively (Terras) to elements t_w(x) = (3^{a(w)}x + c(w))/2^k of the FREE monoid ⟨t_0,t_1⟩⁺ ⊂ A; a T-cycle with parity word w is exactly Fix(t_w) ∈ Z_{>0}. Key exact translation (verified, elementary): since 2^k−3^a is coprime to 6, Fix(t_w) ∈ Z ⟺ Fix(t_w) ∈ Z[1/6]; and "Fix ∈ Z[1/6]" is a conjugacy-type invariant in G(R;Z[1/6],⟨2,3⟩) (the group preserves Z[1/6] and acts transitively on it), so "no nontrivial cycles" ⟺ the free monoid ⟨t_0,t_1⟩⁺ avoids the set of elements conjugate in G to maps with a positive Z[1/6]-fixed point (excluding the trivial cycle words). For divergence/reachability: Kohl's G_T = ⟨τ_{0(2),1(2)}, τ_{1(2),2(4)}, τ_{1(4),2(6)}⟩ ⊂ CT_{2,3}(Z) is transitive on N_0 iff Collatz holds; class transpositions with moduli 2^a3^b are precisely prefix-exchange homeomorphisms of the Cantor space Z_2×Z_3, so CT_{2,3}(Z) is a "(2,3)-adic V" (topological full group of the clopen-prefix groupoid) with Z embedded as a dense invariant countable set, and CT_{2}(Z) ≅ Thompson's V is the finite-state (pure 2-adic) degeneration. Collatz = connectivity of the Schreier (action) graph of three explicit involutions on N_0 inside this group. The correspondence 2-adic↔V↔finite-state vs (2,3)-adic↔CT_{2,3}↔non-finite-state matches our program's exhaustion of ≤7-state regular divergence certificates and Stérin–Woods base-conversion hardness.
strongest_adjacent_theorems: Kohl 2017 (J. Group Theory 20:1025–1030): G_T = ⟨τ_{0(2),1(2)}, τ_{1(2),2(4)}, τ_{1(4),2(6)}⟩ acts transitively on N_0 iff the Collatz conjecture holds; the groups CT_P(Z) (class transpositions with moduli having prime factors in P∋2) are simple and finitely generated for finite P; CT_{2}(Z) ≅ Thompson's V (Higman's G_{2,1}), and the intersection of the CT_P series is V.

Hyde–Skipper–Zaremsky 2026 (arXiv:2605.20564): (Thm A) every action graph (Schreier graph) of a finitely generated subgroup of V acting on an orbit in Cantor space is quasi-isometric to a tree — proved via uniform generation of H_1 of the graph; (Thm B) semiconjugacy rigidity for locally-moving FG-filtered line groups embedded in V; (application) Stein's group F_{2,3} does NOT embed in V.

Stein 1992 (Trans. AMS 332) + Bieri–Strebel (AMS Surveys & Monographs 215, 2016): finiteness properties, presentations, and a complete structure/conjugacy toolkit for the groups G(I; Z[1/6], ⟨2,3⟩) of PL homeomorphisms with slopes 2^a3^b; Wladis (arXiv:0811.3036) solves the word problem and computes the metric for Thompson–Stein groups; Thompson's F is exponentially distorted in them.

Guba–Sapir 1997 (Memoirs AMS, Diagram Groups) + Belk–Matucci 2014: F is the diagram group over ⟨x|x=x²⟩ with complete rewriting normal forms; strand/annular diagrams give linear-time conjugacy algorithms in F, T, V — the normal-form calculus the (2,3) world now gets via Molyneux 2026 (arXiv:2602.08748, tree pairs for algebraic Bieri–Strebel groups) and Li 2023 (arXiv:2312.07375, Stein groups as topological full groups of étale groupoids, with reconstruction rigidity à la Matui/Rubin).

Bleak–Cameron–Olukoya (arXiv:2004.08478): the automorphism group of the one-sided full shift is exactly the group of bi-synchronizing rational transducer homeomorphisms — the precise boundary of finite-state symmetry for the 2-shift to which T|_{Z_2} is conjugate (Lagarias 1985).

Applegate–Lagarias 2006 (Adv. Appl. Math / arXiv:math/0411140): the multiplicative 3x+1 semigroup generated by {(2k+1)/(3k+2)} ∪ {2} equals all positive rationals a/b in lowest terms with 3∤b — proving Farkas's weak 3x+1 conjecture; the one nontrivial theorem ever proved about a Collatz monoid.
the_push: Two channels, stated honestly. (A) Cycle calculus: the reformulation "no nontrivial cycle ⟺ ⟨t_0,t_1⟩⁺ avoids G-conjugates of Z[1/6]-fixed-point elements" is exact but, by itself, repackages the classical divisibility (2^k−3^a) | c(w); the group theory only adds value where the (2,3) world has genuinely new invariants. It has two: (i) the word metric of F_{2,3}/G(R;Z[1/6],⟨2,3⟩) (Wladis), whose distortion estimates are quantitative shadows of |2^k−3^a| lower bounds (the same Baker/Rhin linear-forms-in-logs inputs that power Simons–de Weger and Hercher) — a group-geometric derivation of any |2^k−3^a| bound would be genuinely new, but information currently flows the other way; (ii) the character/BNSR geometry of F_{2,3} (Zaremsky 2012.05000): the data (k, a(w)) of a parity word is exactly the image of t_w under the canonical characters (log-slope homomorphisms), and cycle words live near the irrational direction k·log2 = a·log3 in character space, where the BNSR invariant detects exceptional (non-F_∞-kernel) directions — a structural rhyme worth one focused look, not more. (B) Divergence via action-graph geometry — the real prize. Kohl's equivalence has sat unused since 2017 because permutation-group tools stall; HSZ 2026 just built exactly the missing tool for V. Extend HSZ Theorem A from V = CT_{2}(Z) to finitely generated subgroups of CT_{2,3}(Z) acting on orbits in Z_2×Z_3 (class transpositions are prefix swaps on the two-sorted (2,3)-adic tree, so their uniform-H_1-generation argument plausibly transfers). Consequence: EVERY orbit of Kohl's G_T on N_0 — one per hypothetical divergent trajectory or extra cycle — carries a Schreier graph quasi-isometric to a tree with uniform constants coming from three explicit involutions of bounded displacement ratio. Combine with density facts (Tao 2019 gives the orbit of 1 full logarithmic density; any extra orbit has log-density ~0): a hypothetical divergent component is then an unbounded uniform quasi-tree drawn on a log-density-zero subset of N whose vertex set is closed under x↦2x-type moves — a geometric/arithmetic tension that no existing method expresses, and a natural home for a new class of impossibility results complementing our regular-certificate exhaustion (which killed the finite-state versions of such components). Honest ceiling: this will not prove Collatz; it could prove "no divergent orbit whose action graph has property X" for a geometrically natural X invisible to number theory, and the CT_{2,3} quasi-tree theorem is publishable in the Thompson literature independently of Collatz.
first_move: Two-track, both startable today. (1) Lean anchor lemma (1–2 days): formalize the dictionary — (a) ⟨t_0,t_1⟩⁺ is free (parity-vector uniqueness, Terras); (b) for nonempty w, writing t_w(x)=(3^a x+c)/2^k, gcd(2^k−3^a, 6)=1, hence Fix(t_w) ∈ Z ⟺ Fix(t_w) ∈ Z[1/6]; (c) therefore no-nontrivial-cycles ⟺ the free monoid inside G(R;Z[1/6],⟨2,3⟩) avoids the conjugacy-saturated set of elements with positive Z[1/6]-fixed points. This is the certified foundation of the calculus. (2) 32-core computation + theorem attempt (the real move): implement Kohl's three involutions a=τ_{0(2),1(2)}, b=τ_{1(2),2(4)}, c=τ_{1(4),2(6)} (each is an explicit affine swap of residue classes, O(1) per application); BFS the Schreier graph of ⟨a,b,c⟩ from 0 on N_0 out to ~10^9 vertices; measure HSZ's quantities directly — the uniform H_1-generation constant L (max length of independent short cycles), the quasi-tree (bottleneck) constant, growth and ends profile — and compare against the same statistics for the 5-transitive control group G_5 = ⟨a,b,τ_{0(3),2(3)}⟩ that Kohl notes is provably transitive; in parallel, attempt the proof that action graphs of f.g. subgroups of CT_{2,3}(Z) on Z_2×Z_3-orbits are quasi-trees by adapting HSZ's uniform-H_1 argument to (2,3)-adic prefix swaps. The computation immediately falsifies or calibrates the conjectured theorem, and any observed unbounded-L behavior for G_T vs bounded-L for G_5 is itself a publishable structural distinction pinpointing where Collatz-hardness lives group-theoretically.
payoff_odds: Realistic best case: (i) the CT_{2,3} quasi-tree/action-graph theorem — a solid, independently publishable result in the Thompson–Stein literature (I estimate 30–50% it goes through by adapting HSZ, whose proof is only 6 pages and uses prefix combinatorics that class transpositions share); (ii) on top of it, a genuinely new no-divergence criterion of the form "no extra orbit with geometric property X," complementary to our DFA-certificate exhaustion — maybe 5–10% that X is strong enough to exclude anything number theory can't already. Likely case: a clean certified dictionary (cycles = conjugacy avoidance in a Bieri–Strebel group; divergence = extra quasi-tree orbits of three involutions), high-quality Schreier-graph data distinguishing G_T from provably-transitive cousins, and a bridge paper that gives the Thompson school a concrete reason to aim its rapidly-improving (2,3) technology (HSZ 2026, Li 2023, Molyneux 2026) at Collatz. Odds of a real dent in Collatz itself: under 2% — the divisibility core (2^k−3^a) | c(w) and orbit-connectivity are exactly the parts conjugacy invariants and QI geometry are blind to, and Kohl's decade of RCWA work shows the reformulation alone does not bite. The honest value proposition is: this is the one adjacent field whose brand-new tools (May 2026) exactly fit a 2017 reformulation nobody has revisited, and the first move is cheap.

prior_art.ref: Stefan Kohl, 'The Collatz conjecture in a group theoretic context', J. Group Theory 20 (2017) 1025–1030
prior_art.what: THE closest prior art. Constructs G_T = ⟨τ_{0(2),1(2)}, τ_{1(2),2(4)}, τ_{1(4),2(6)}⟩, transitive on N_0 iff Collatz holds; shows G_T ⊂ CT_{2,3}(Z), a finitely generated simple group in a series CT_P(Z) whose intersection is Thompson's V (= CT_{2}(Z), noted by McDermott). Backed by Kohl's RCWA GAP package and J. Symbolic Computation algorithms (tame vs wild dichotomy). NOT done: any use of Thompson-school geometry (quasi-trees, semiconjugacy, BNSR, full-group rigidity) on the equivalence; it has sat as a bare reformulation since 2017.

prior_art.ref: Peter M. Hines, 'From a conjecture of Collatz to Thompson's group F, via a conjunction of Girard', arXiv:2202.04443 (2022)
prior_art.what: Realizes Thompson's F as Conway-style congruential bijections of N generated from Collatz's ORIGINAL 1932 permutation plus Girard's Geometry-of-Interaction conjunction; the F-generator x_0 is the associator built from the Collatz bijection. Categorical observation about the OCC, not the 3x+1 map. NOT done: any dynamics, any number theory, any word/conjugacy calculus; Remark 14 explicitly leaves open the structure of the congruential group containing F.

prior_art.ref: James Hyde, Rachel Skipper, Matthew Zaremsky, 'Action graphs, semiconjugacy, and non-embedding in Thompson's group V', arXiv:2605.20564 (May 2026)
prior_art.what: Not about Collatz, but the newest and sharpest tool on the bridge: action graphs of f.g. subgroups of V on Cantor orbits are quasi-trees (via uniform H_1 generation); semiconjugacy rigidity for line groups in V; Stein's F_{2,3} does NOT embed in V. Establishes that the (2,3)-slope world is provably outside the 2-adic V world. NOT done: any CT_{2,3}(Z)/(2,3)-adic version, any arithmetic application.

prior_art.ref: Tristan Stérin, Damien Woods, 'The Collatz process embeds a base conversion algorithm' (RP 2020), arXiv:2007.06979
prior_art.what: Collatz process embeds base-3→base-2 conversion; exhibits the dual finite-state transducers (3x+1 in binary; x/2 in base 30, dual to it). Explains mechanistically why single branches are finite-state but the parity-conditional composition is not. NOT done: no group theory, no Thompson connection drawn.

prior_art.ref: David Applegate, Jeffrey Lagarias, 'The 3x+1 semigroup', J. Number Theory / arXiv:math/0411140 (2006); Farkas 2005
prior_art.what: The 'Collatz monoid' prior art on the multiplicative side: the semigroup generated by {(2k+1)/(3k+2)} ∪ {2} equals {a/b in lowest terms : 3∤b}, proving Farkas's weak 3x+1 conjecture (Wild Numbers Theorem). NOT done: the affine (parity-word) monoid inside Aff(Q)/Bieri–Strebel groups — the object our bridge uses — has no analogous theorem.

prior_art.ref: Takehiko Mori, 'Application of Operator Theory for the Collatz Conjecture', arXiv:2411.08084 (2024)
prior_art.what: Reformulates Collatz via a C*-algebra isomorphic to Cuntz's O_2 built from first-return maps of the Collatz dynamics. Adjacent to Thompson land (V sits inside O_2 machinery) but purely operator-theoretic. NOT done: no group-theoretic normal forms, no link to V/F_{2,3} made.

prior_art.ref: Belk–Bleak–Matucci school: 'Rational embeddings of hyperbolic groups' arXiv:1711.08369; Bleak–Cameron–Olukoya arXiv:2004.08478; Belk–Bleak RIMS Kôkyûroku 2265
prior_art.what: The rational-group/transducer technology (finite-state homeomorphisms of Cantor space; shift automorphisms = bi-synchronizing transducers). VERIFIED NEGATIVE: contrary to the recollection in our task brief, I found no BBM publication on the (2,3) base-change homeomorphism or Collatz — the RIMS survey greps clean for Collatz/Kohl/base-change. This part of the hole is genuinely open.

prior_art.ref: (2,3)-group structure theory with zero Collatz contact: Stein TAMS 1992; Bieri–Strebel AMS Monographs 215 (2016); Wladis arXiv:0811.3036; Zaremsky arXiv:2012.05000 (BNSR of F_{2,3}); Xin Li arXiv:2312.07375 (Stein groups as topological full groups); Molyneux arXiv:2602.08748 (tree pairs for algebraic Bieri–Strebel groups)
prior_art.what: Complete word-problem, metric, finiteness, character (BNSR), full-group and emerging normal-form technology for exactly the slope-2^a3^b groups our bridge needs. NOT done: none of these papers mentions Collatz; searches for 'Collatz + topological full group / étale groupoid / Bieri–Strebel / Stein group' return nothing.
